title: Nephrodium ciliatum
taxonomy: Nephrodium ciliatum is a genus of extinct ferns in the family Matoniaceae, which is part of the class Polypodiopsida. This classification is based on the plant's fossil record and morphological characteristics. The Matoniaceae family is a group of extinct ferns that were likely to have been part of the tropical and subtropical ecosystems. Nephrodium ciliatum is often found in association with other fossil ferns and plants in the same geological formations. Its classification as a member of the Polypodiopsida class highlights its relationship to modern ferns and its place in the fern evolutionary history.
uses: Nephrodium ciliatum is an ancient plant species that has been utilized for various purposes, including its supposed medicinal properties. side_effects:
Nephrodium ciliatum is an extinct species of fern, and as such, it does not have any reported side effects in humans or animals since it is no longer used in modern medicine. However, it's essential to note that some species of ferns, in general, can cause irritation or allergic reactions in sensitive individuals when handled or inhaled. 

The plant's spores and leaves might trigger reactions such as skin redness, itching, or respiratory issues in people with fern allergies. If you suspect you have an allergy or sensitivity to ferns, it's best to consult a medical professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. In most cases, the symptoms will be mild and temporary, but severe reactions are possible in rare instances.
intro: Nephrodium ciliatum, also known as the ciliate maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful in alleviating conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium ciliatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Nephrodium ciliatum is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
